name: ALPR frame extractor worker (fcamera -> JPEG frames)
summary: Background worker that samples frames from each new fcamera segment at the configured FPS, paired with per-frame millisecond offsets so detections can be correlated with the route's GPS timeline. Producer half of the ALPR pipeline; alpr-detection-worker is the consumer. Triggered by the same segment-finalized signal that the existing transcoder + event-detector workers use -- check internal/worker/event_detector.go and internal/worker/transcoder.go for the exact wire-up pattern.
acceptance_criteria: New worker file internal/worker/alpr_extractor.go. Started from cmd/server/workers.go, gated on the runtime alpr_enabled flag (not env var).

Toggle responsiveness: when the runtime flag flips false the worker stops dequeuing new segments within ~30 s; when it flips true, it resumes from the queue. Implementation can be poll-based (check the flag every ~10s + at the top of each segment loop) or event-based via a settings-change channel; either is acceptable, document choice in code.

Trigger: subscribes to the same segment-finalized signal used by event-detector. Match the existing pattern; do NOT invent a new signaling mechanism. Look at how cmd/server/workers.go starts event-detector and follow.

For each fcamera segment file at <storage>/<dongle>/<route>/<seg>/fcamera.hevc: call ffmpeg via os/exec with `-i fcamera.hevc -vf 'fps=<configured>' -f image2pipe -vcodec mjpeg -` to stream JPEG frames on stdout. Wrap stderr to a rate-limited logger so transient ffmpeg warnings don't flood logs.

Compute frame_offset_ms = round(frame_index * 1000 / fps) relative to segment start. Emit {dongle_id, route, segment, frame_offset_ms, jpeg_bytes []byte} onto a buffered channel (capacity ~32; tunable via env ALPR_EXTRACTOR_BUFFER, default 32) consumed by alpr-detection-worker.
Concurrency: ALPR_EXTRACTOR_CONCURRENCY env (default 1). With CPU-only ALPR engine this should stay 1 to avoid starving the engine; on GPU users may bump to 2-4. Honor the existing config plumbing.

Idempotency: skip a segment if alpr_segment_progress.processed_at_extractor is non-null. Mark it set on successful completion (all frames pushed to channel, ffmpeg exited cleanly). Do NOT mark on failure.
Failure handling: ffmpeg non-zero exit => log warn with stderr tail, do NOT mark processed, move on to next segment. Don't crash the worker on a single bad segment.
Graceful shutdown: on context cancel, stop the ffmpeg child, drain in-flight frames, close the output channel, return.
Backpressure: if the downstream channel is full, the extractor blocks (this is the intended behavior -- prevents unbounded memory growth when the engine is slow).

Metrics: register Prometheus counters/histograms via internal/metrics: alpr_frames_extracted_total{result=ok|error}, alpr_extractor_segment_seconds (histogram), alpr_extractor_queue_depth (gauge of channel length).

Tests with a small fixture HEVC under internal/worker/testdata/: extract at 2 fps and assert correct frame count + monotonic increasing offsets. Test the idempotency path (second run on same segment is a no-op). Test cancel during extraction (no goroutine leak).
